California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornrnission
I{ay 30, 199 O
Rita wal,ters
Board of Education
city of Los Angeles
P.O. Box 3307
Los Angeles, CA 9 0051
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-9o-3 og
Dear Ms. walters:
This is in response to your letter requestj,ng advj-ce regard-
ing your responsibilities as a member of the Los Angeles Unified
SchooI District Board of Education under the ne$, rrpersonal usert
provisions of the Political Reforn Act (the "a61"1-. 1/
OUESTION
May canpai.gn funds be used to pay for your travel, acconmoda-
tions and conference fees for a conference concerning middle
schools as a method to relieve school overcrowding, sponsored by
the california League of Middle schools and the Hawaii Association
of Middle School-s?
CONCLUSION
Expenditures made for a conference for public officials
intended to irnprove their abj.lity to perforrn their officia] 
duties
and to provide inforrnation to be used in the performance of the
official's 
governmental duties are directly related to a
governmental purpose. consequently, campaign funds nay be used to
pay to attend the conference, Further, use of campaign funds to
pay for ordinary and necessary travel expenses to and from the
conference is also perrnitted.
Governnent Code Sections 810o0-91015. AlL statutory refer-
ences are to the covernment code unless otherwise indicated. 
com-
rnission reg'ulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4.O8O7 . (916) 3 22.5660


Our File No. A-90-308
Page 2
FACTS
You have asked rrrhether you may use campaign funds to pay for
travel, accommodations and program fees for a conference on niddle
schools offered by the california League of Middle Schools and the
Hawaii Association of Middle schools. 
The middle school is an
alternative to a two year junior high school system in which the
junior high school is replaced with a three year rniddle school.
The restructuring of the school system results in less crowding at
the high school 1eveI. The conference is scheduled to run frorn
June 27 through ,fune 29 and will concern such topics as the
scheduling and the characteristics and instructional practices of
middle schools.
ANALYSIS
Effective January L, 7990, the Act was amended by Senate Bil-l-
1431 to include new provisions that regulate the appropriate use
of canpaign funds. (Section 85800, et seq., the rrpersonal usetr
Iaw. ) The purpose of the legislation was rrto ensure that
candidates, elected officers and the peopl,e clearly recognize ac-
ceptable and unacceptable uses of campaign funds." (Senate EIec-
tions Committee Analysis of sB 1431.)
The general rule of the ne\., personaL use 1aw is that any
expenditure of carnpaign funds must be, at a ninimuu, reasonably
related to a political, 
Iegislative, or governmental purpose.
(Section 85801.) Hoerever, where an expenditure confers a
substantial personal benefit on the candidate or officeholder, the
expenditure must be direclly related to a political, 
legislative,
or governrnental purpose.2/ (section 85801.)
Proqram Fees
where campaign funds are used to pay a progran fee for your
attendance at an educational conference, you have recej'ved a
personaL benefit equivalent to the payment ' Accordinq to the
iacts you provided; the tuition for the conference is $100.
conseqiently, for the expenditure of campaign funds to be perrnis-
sible there must be a diiect relationship between the expenditure
and a politicat, 
legislative, or governmental purpose. (Section
85801. )
Since the infornation you obtain at the conference offered by
the California League of tfiadle Schools and Hawaii Association of
Middle schools directly relates to your rol,e as a board member of
the Los Angeles Unj.f ied School District and will be used in the
perforrnanc6 of your governmental duties, it appears there is a
hirect relation-ship between the expenditure of carnpaign funds for
zl A substantial personal benefit is defined as
benefit to the candidate or officeholder of $10o
tion 85800 (b) (2) . )
a direct personal
or nore. 
( Sec-


our File No. A-9 0-3 08
Page 3
the conference and a governmental purpose associate with your
elected office. 
consequently, canpaign funds may be used to pay
prolrram fees to attend the conference.
Travel and Accommodations
section 85802 (a) (1) provides:
(a) Campaign funds shall not be used to pay
or reimburse the candidate, the el-ected officer, or
emptoyees or staff of the campaign committee or the
elected offj,cer's governmental agency for travel
expenses and necessary accommodations except lrhen
these expenditures are directlv related to a
political-, Iectislative, or qoverrulental purpose.
(1) For purposes of this sectj-on, payments or
reimbursements for travel and necessary acconmoda-
tions sha11 be considered as directly reLated to a
political, 
legislative, or governmental purpose if
the paylDents would meet standards slmilar to the
standards of the Internal Revenue Service pursuant
to Sections 162 and 274 of the fnternal Revenue
Code for deductions of travel expenses under the
federal income tax Iaw.
(Enphasis added. )
According to Section 85802(a) (1), the use of campaign funds
for paynents or reimbursements for travel- and necessary accommoda-
tions is directly related to a political, 
Iegislative, or
governmental purpose if the Payments meet standards sinilar to the
standards for the deduction of travel' expenses pursuant to
Internal Revenue Code Sections 152 and 274.
Internal Revenue Code section 162(a) (2) sets forth a general
rule which permits the deduction of all ordinary and necessary
traveling expenses while aalay from hone in the pursui't of a trade
or business. According to your facts the travel expenses are
ordinary and necessary expenses for a governmental purpose which
are associated with your official 
duties. 
Consequently, use of
canpaign funds for the travel- expenses is pernitted.s/
Ptease note, hovrever that accommodations outside the duration
of the conference are personaL expenses that should be paid with
3/ It should be recognized that the commission has recently as-
surned jurisdiction over the laws regarding the perrnissible uses of
canpai;n funds. In the near future the Cornmission will begin
considering and approving regulations interpreting these laws.
Therefore, it is possible that the Conmission may adopt regula-
tions in the future which nay be at variance with the advice given
in this letter.


Our File No. A-90-308
Page 4
personal funds. Thus, after the conference ends on the 29th of
June, accornmodations for any subseguent days may not be Paid with
canpaign funds.
If you have any further guestions regarding this natter,
please feel free to contact ne at (916) 322-590]--
sincerely,
scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral counsel
